● In this system, drugs obtained only from herbal, animal and mineral sources are used for medication. Sometimes, these drugs are used alone, and sometimes in the form of a compound of various drugs. They may be subjected to physico-chemical processing but without breaking up the unnatural character. Thus, the Unani system of medicine uses only natural substances in treatment. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● The basic framework of this system is based on the Hippocratic theory of four Humours, according to which any disturbance in the equilibrium of humours causes disease and therefore the treatment aims at restoring the humoral equilibrium. The system also believes that Medicatrix Naturae (Tabiat Mudabbira-i-Badan) is the supreme power, which controls all the physiological functions of the body, provides resistance against diseases and helps in healing naturally. o Unani medicine is based upon the theory of humours, which presupposes the presence of four humours in the body: dam (blood), balgham (phlegm), safra (yellow bile), and sawda (black bile). So, statement 3 is correct. So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

opt.t: virus that causes respiratory infections in humans
bacterial strain often picked up by surgery patients in hospitals
gram-positive bacterium commonly found in soil and water
fungal pathogen responsible for skin infections

q.t: Klebsiella’, recently seen in the news, is a:

so.t:
● Klebsiella is a type of gram-negative bacteria that can cause different types of healthcare-associated infections, including pneumonia, bloodstream infections, wound or surgical site infections and meningitis.
● Klebsiella bacteria are normally found in the human intestines (where they do not cause disease). They are also found in human stool (faeces).
● In healthcare settings, Klebsiella infections commonly occur among sick patients who are receiving treatment for other conditions. Patients whose care requires devices like ventilators (breathing machines) or intravenous (vein) catheters, and patients who are taking long courses of certain antibiotics are most at risk for Klebsiella infections. Healthy people usually do not get Klebsiella infections.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: It is a type of artificial intelligence that mimics human thought processes and can understand, reason and learn from data.
It is a form of quantum computing that relies on the principles of superposition and entanglement to process information.
It is a term used to describe traditional computing methods that follow a linear, rule-based approach to problem-solving.
It is a form of industrial automation that is primarily concerned with automating physical tasks, such as manufacturing and robotics.

q.t: Which one of the following best describes the term “cognitive computing”?

so.t:
● A cognitive computing system is trained to simulate human thought processes and learn based on interactions and outcomes rather than simply presenting information like one sees when entering a keyword into a search engine.
● It’s a cognitive system that can act almost like a human in its capacity to answer questions in a way that feels natural and with remarkable speed and accuracy, reacting quickly and in real time.
● Cognitive computing uses a massive quantity of information, including the complexities of an evolving language and provides answers and recommendations that are supported by existing data.
● Adaptation is one of the primary characteristics and advantages of cognitive computing, as these technologies can learn from what they’ve already seen or done.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Piezoelectric Materials (PM) are materials that can generate an electrical charge when mechanical stress is applied. Which of the following are classified as Piezoelectric Materials?
1. Bones
2. Silicon
3. Quartz
4. Sugar
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● Piezoelectricity is the property of a substance to generate electricity if a stress such as squeezing, bending or twisting is applied to it. Materials that exhibit the piezoelectric effect are known as Piezoelectric materials. They also demonstrate the inverse piezoelectric effect (also called the reverse or converse piezoelectric effect). The inverse piezoelectric effect is the internal generation of mechanical strain in response to an applied electrical field.
● Bone is a multidirectional and anisotropic piezoelectric material that exhibits an electrical microenvironment. So, point 1 is correct.
● Quartz is a naturally occurring piezoelectric material that has been used for its piezoelectric properties. Its durability and availability make it ideal for use in oscillators, watches, and communication equipment. So, point 3 is correct.
● Ceramics exhibit piezoelectric effects. Lead zirconate titanate (PZT), barium titanate (BT), and strontium titanate (ST) are the most widely used piezoelectric ceramic materials.
● Materials like Rochelle salts (potassium sodium tartrate), many plastics, table sugar, etc also exhibit piezoelectric effect. So, point 4 is correct.
● Silicon is not a piezoelectric material. So, point 2 is not correct. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which of the following actions under Directive Principles of State Policy cannot be declared void on the grounds of contravention of Article 14 and Article 19 of the Constitution of India?
1. Provision of right to an adequate means of livelihood equally for men and women
2. Distribution of material resources of the community to subserve the common good
3. Avoidance of concentration of wealth and means of production
4. Providing equal pay for equal work for both men and women
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
● Article 31C of the Constitution of India (inserted by the 25th Constitutional Amendment Act of 1971) contained the following two provisions:
○ No law that seeks to implement the socialistic directive principles specified in Article 39(b) or (c) shall be void on the ground of contravention of the Fundamental Rights conferred by Article 14 (equality before law and equal protection of laws) or Article 19 (protection of six rights in respect of speech, assembly, movement, etc.).
■ Article 39(b) states that the State should direct its policy towards securing ownership and control of the material resources of the community that are so distributed as best to subserve the common good. So, point 2 is correct.
■ Article 39(c) states that the State should direct its policy towards securing the operation of the economic system that does not result in the concentration of wealth and means of production to the common detriment. So, point 3 is correct.
○ No law containing a declaration that it is for giving effect to such policy shall be questioned in any court on the ground that it does not give effect to such a policy.
● In the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), the Supreme Court declared the above second provision of Article 31C as unconstitutional and invalid on the ground that judicial review is a basic feature of the Constitution and hence, cannot be taken away. However, the first provision of Article 31C was held to be constitutional and valid.
○ The 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act (1976) extended the scope of the above first provision of Article 31C by including within its protection any law to implement any of the Directive Principles specified in Part IV of the Constitution and not merely in Article 39 (b) or (c). However, this extension was declared as unconstitutional and invalid by the Supreme Court in the Minerva Mills case (1980)
● Article 39(a) of the Constitution of India states that the State shall, in particular, direct its policy towards securing that the citizens, men and women equally, have the right to an adequate means of livelihood. This is not a part of the subject matter of Article 31C of the Constitution of India. So, point 1 is not correct.
● Article 39(d) of the Constitution of India states that the State shall, in particular, direct its policy towards securing that there is equal pay for equal work for both men and women. This is not a part of the subject matter of Article 31C of the Constitution of India. So, point 4 is not correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the appointment of High Court judges:
1. The proposal for the appointment of Chief Justice of a High Court is initiated by the Union Minister of Law and Justice.
2. The Chief Justice of the High Court can make an appointment of an additional judge in the High Court only when there is a vacancy for a permanent judge.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● Every High Court consists of a Chief Justice and such other judges as the President may from time to time deem necessary to appoint. Initiation of the proposal for the appointment of Chief Justice of a High Court would be by the Chief Justice of India. The Chief Justice of India, in consultation with the two senior most Judges of the Supreme Court will send the recommendations to the Union Minister of Law, Justice and Company Affairs. So, statement 1 is not correct.
○ After receipt of the recommendation of the Chief Justice of India, the Union Minister of Law and Justice would obtain the views of the concerned State Government. The Union Minister of Law will then submit proposals to the Prime Minister, who will then advise the President as to the selection.
● Article 224 of the Constitution provides that additional judges can be appointed by the President under clause (1) of Article 224 of the Constitution. The procedure for the appointment of the additional judge will be the same as the appointment of a permanent judge. The Chief Justice of the High Court, however, should not make a recommendation for appointment of an additional judge when a vacancy of a permanent Judge is available in that High Court. So, statement 2 is not correct.
○ Acting judge: The President can also appoint a duly qualified person as an acting judge of a High Court when a judge of that High Court (other than the Chief Justice) is unable to perform the duties of his office due to absence or any other reason; or appointed to act temporarily as Chief Justice of that High Court. An acting judge holds office until the permanent judge resumes his office. However, both the additional or acting judge cannot hold office after attaining the age of 62 years.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following situations:
1. Anti-secular politics
2. Wilful refusal of the State Government to discharge its constitutional obligations
3. Hung Assembly
4. Maladministration in the State
In how many of the above situations the President's rule can be imposed in a State?

so.t:
● The Supreme Court of India in SR Bommai Case (1994) held that secularism is one of the ‘basic features’ of the Constitution of India. Hence, a State Government pursuing anti-secular politics is liable to action under Article 356. So, point 1 is correct.
● Imposition of the President’s Rule in a State would be proper in the following situations:
○ Where after general elections to the assembly, no party secures a majority, that is, ‘Hung Assembly’. So, point 3 is correct.
○ Where the party having a majority in the assembly declines to form a ministry and the Governor cannot find a coalition ministry commanding a majority in the assembly.
○ Internal subversion where, for example, a government is deliberately acting against the Constitution and the law or is fomenting a violent revolt.
○ Physical breakdown where the government wilfully refuses to discharge its constitutional obligations endangering the security of the state. So, point 2 is correct.
● The imposition of the President’s Rule in a state would be improper under the following situations:
○ Where a ministry resigns or is dismissed on losing majority support in the assembly and the Governor recommends imposition of President’s Rule without probing the possibility of forming an alternative ministry.
○ Internal disturbances not amounting to internal subversion or physical breakdown.
○ Maladministration in the State or allegations of corruption against the ministry or stringent financial exigencies of the State. So, point 4 is not correct.

q.t:
With reference to the alliance of the political parties in India, consider the following statements:
1. They are recognised as statutory entities under the Representation of People Act, 1951.
2. They are mandated to get approval of their legal name from the Election Commission of India.
3. The ceiling on parliamentary poll expenditure of the alliance of political parties is regulated by the Election Commission of India.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Many a time, leading political parties reach out to regional parties to form a collaboration on a common agenda or goal in order to create a Government. Such an arrangement is called a political alliance.
● Kerala High Court in Dr George Joseph Themplangad v Union of India & Ors, 2021 held that political alliances are not legal entities under the Representation of People Act, 1951. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● Political party alliances are not mandated to get approval of their legal name from the Election Commission of India. Kerala High court stated in the above judgement that there are no statutory provisions mandating the Election Commission of India (ECI) to regulate functioning of the political parties. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● Unlike the limit of election expenditure imposed on a contesting candidate by the Union of India through the ECI, there is no limit on the expenditure that a party can incur during its election campaigns. Recently, The ceiling on parliamentary poll expenditure of contesting candidates has been raised from 70 lakh to 95 lakh rupees in bigger states and 54 lakh to 75 lakhs in smaller states. So, statement 3 is not correct. So, none of the above statements are correct. Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to De-notified tribes, consider the following statements:
1. All of these tribes are categorised under the Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST) and Other Backward Caste (OBC) category by the Government.
2. The National Commission for De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (NCDNT) works under the Ministry of Tribal Affairs.
3. The Justice Rohini Commission was constituted in 2006 to look into the grievances of these tribes.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
De-notified tribes:
● These are communities who are the most vulnerable and deprived. Denotified tribes (DNTs) are communities that were ‘notified’ as being ‘born criminal’ during the British regime under a series of laws starting with the Criminal Tribes Act of 1871. Nomadic and semi-nomadic communities are defined as those who move from one place to another rather than living at one place all the time.
● While a number of these tribes are categorised under Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST) and Other Backward Caste (OBC), many are not. The standing committee report in Parliament has cited a statement by the Secretary, Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, that 269 DNT communities are not covered under any reserved categories. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The government therefore set up the Development and Welfare Board for De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Communities (DWBDNC) under the Societies Registration Act, 1860 under the aegis of the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment for the purpose of implementing welfare programmes.
● A National Commission for De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (NCDNT) was constituted by the Government of India in 2014 to prepare a state-wise list of castes belonging to De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes. The Commission is set up under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● A National Commission for De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (NCDNT) was constituted in 2006. It was headed by Balkrishna Sidram Renke and submitted its report in 2008. So, statement 3 is not correct. ○ The Justice G. Rohini-headed Commission was formed in 2017 for the sub-categorisation of Other Backward Classes (OBC) caste groups. So, none of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs

How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?

so.t:
● Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM-JANMAN): It is designed to provide support to tribal communities. It is aimed at providing Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) households and habitations with basic facilities such as safe housing, clean drinking water and sanitation, improved access to education, health and nutrition, road and telecom connectivity, and sustainable livelihood opportunities. So, pair 1 is correctly matched.
● Pradhan Mantri Uchchatar Shiksha Abhiyan (PM-USHA): It is a centrally sponsored programme launched in 2013. It aims at providing strategic funding to eligible state higher educational institutions. It places greater emphasis on the improvement of the quality of teaching-learning processes in order to produce employable and competitive graduates, post-graduates and PhDs. So, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
● Pradhan Mantri Anusuchit Jaati Abhuyday Yojana (PM- AJAY): It is a merged scheme of 03 Centrally Sponsored Scheme namely Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAGY), Special Central Assistance to Scheduled Castes Sub Plan (SCA to SCSP) and Babu Jagjivan Ram Chhatrawas Yojana (BJRCY). It has been implemented since 2021-22 with an aim to reduce poverty of the Scheduled Caste (SC) communities by generation of additional employment opportunities through Skill development, income generating schemes and other initiatives. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
● PM Mega Integrated Textile Region and Apparel (PM MITRA): It aims to strengthen the Indian textile industry by way of enabling scale of operations, reduce logistics cost by housing the entire value chain at one location, attract investment, generate employment and augment export potential. The scheme envisages to develop an integrated large scale and modern industrial infrastructure facility for total value-chain of the textile industry for example, spinning, weaving, processing, garmenting, textile manufacturing, processing & printing machinery industry. So, pair 4 is not correctly matched. So, only two of the above pairs are correctly matched.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Airborne forces cannot successfully capture and hold strategic objectives without rapid ground force linkup
Allied air superiority alone cannot guarantee success against entrenched defensive positions
Intelligence failures regarding enemy strength and disposition can negate operational advantages
Logistical constraints in supplying advancing armies across destroyed infrastructure

q.t: The strategic failure of Operation Market Garden (September 1944) demonstrated which fundamental principle of combined military operations?

so.t: Explanation: Operation Market Garden, Field Marshal Montgomery's ambitious plan to end the war in 1944 by securing bridges across the Netherlands into Germany, failed primarily because airborne forces could not hold their objectives long enough for ground forces to arrive. The operation involved the largest airborne assault in history, with paratroopers capturing bridges at Eindhoven, Nijmegen, and Arnhem. While the southern bridges were eventually secured, British 1st Airborne Division at Arnhem faced unexpectedly strong German resistance and held their positions for nine days—far longer than planned—but XXX Corps advancing from the south could not break through in time along the narrow corridor (Hell's Highway). The fundamental flaw was the plan's reliance on rapid ground linkup with isolated airborne units across a 64-mile single-road advance. Option B, while true, doesn't capture the specific failure—Allied air superiority existed but couldn't compensate for the ground force delay. Option C (intelligence failures) was certainly a factor, as Allied intelligence underestimated the presence of II SS Panzer Corps near Arnhem, but this was secondary to the structural problem of the airborne-ground linkup timeline. Option D (logistics) became problematic but was not the primary cause of failure—the operation failed before logistical issues became decisive.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
1. Members of the State Public Service Commission are appointed and removed by the Governor of the State.
2. The Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) is appointed and removed by the President of India.
3. Both Chief Election Commissioner and Election Commissioners can be removed on the same grounds as a judge of the Supreme Court.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Although the Chairperson and members of a State Public Service Commission (SPSC) are appointed by the Governor, they can be removed only by the President (and not by the Governor). The President can remove them on the same grounds and in the same manner as he can remove a Chairperson or a member of the UPSC. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) is appointed by the President of India by a warrant under his hand and seal. The CAG, before taking over her/his office, makes and subscribes before the President an oath or affirmation. S/He can be removed by the President on the basis of a resolution passed to that effect by both the Houses of Parliament with special majority, either on the ground of proved misbehaviour or incapacity. So, statement 2 is correct.
● The Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) is provided with the security of tenure. S/He cannot be removed from her/his office except in the same manner and on the same grounds as a judge of the Supreme Court. Article 324 (5) of the Constitution of India safeguards the CEC from arbitrary removal. However, it does not provide similar protection to the Election Commissioners and it merely says that they cannot be removed from office except on the recommendation of the CEC. So, statement 3 is not correct. So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
With reference to Delegated Legislation in India, consider the following statements:
1. If the Parent Act conferring power on the subordinate authority is itself unconstitutional then the delegated legislation is also unconstitutional.
2. If the Parent Act is Constitutional but the delegated legislation is ultra vires the Constitution, then the delegated
legislation will be declared invalid.
Which of the statements given above is/are n correct?

so.t:
● When the functions of legislation are entrusted to organs other than the legislature, then the legislation made by such an organ is called 'delegated legislation'.
● Delegated legislation is also known as 'sub-ordinated legislation', because the legislative power of the organ which makes it is limited by the statute which confers such power and thus it is valid only if it is within the limits of the statute.
● Under the following circumstances, the delegated legislation can be declared invalid on grounds of substantive ultra vires.
○ Parent Act is unconstitutional: If the Parent Act conferring power on the subordinate authority is itself unconstitutional then the delegated legislation is also unconstitutional or ultra vires. In the case of Chintamanrao Vs. State of M.P, 1951, the Parent Act authorised the Deputy Commissioner (DC) to prohibit manufacturing of bidis in some areas. Court held that the order passed by the DC under the act is ultra vires and even the Parent Act is ultra vires the constitution as it violates the Fundamental Right to carry on any trade, business or any occupation, under Article 19 of the Constitution of India. So, statement 1 is correct.
○ Delegated Legislation is ultra vires the Constitution: If the statute or Parent Act is constitutional but the delegated legislation is ultra vires the Constitution, then the delegated legislation will be declared invalid. Same was held in the case of Air India vs. Nargesh Meerza, 1981, where a regulation was framed by Air India providing that service of an air hostess will be terminated on her first pregnancy, this was held violative of Article 14 of Constitution. So, statement 2 is correct.
○ Delegated legislation is inconsistent with the Parent Act: It is important to note that even if the Parent Act and delegated legislation both are constitutional but the delegated legislation is inconsistent with the Parent Act, then such delegated legislation will be invalid. In Indian Council of Legal Aid & Advice vs. Bar Council of India, 1955, rule was framed by bar council barring enrolment as advocate of a person who had completed 45 years of age. However, the Parent Act only enabled Bar Council to lay down conditions subject to which an advocate shall have right to practice. Thus, the rule was held to be inconsistent with the Parent act.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

opt.t: It refers to the restoration of natural resources like wetlands to help in the management of excess rainwater.
It refers to the development of infrastructure corridors for the safe movement of wildlife.
It refers to human-engineered infrastructure created for the management of water resources.
It is a computing network that seeks to improve and automate the delivery of forest products.

q.t: Which one of the following statements best describes the 'Grey Infrastructure'?

so.t:
● Grey infrastructure refers to traditional human-engineered infrastructure constructed often using traditional materials such as concrete and steel for various services required by society, such as roads, dams, wastewater treatment plants, pipelines, etc.
● Grey infrastructure plays an important role in supporting urbanisation and providing essential services to the population. It also typically refers to components of a centralised approach to water management. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
The short-lived halogens released by the oceans:
1. increase methane’s lifetime in the atmosphere
2. increase the level of water vapour in the atmosphere
3. increase the formation of cooling aerosols in the atmosphere
4. have no impact on ozone level in the troposphere
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
● The six non-metallic elements - fluorine (F), chlorine (Cl), bromine (Br), iodine (I), astatine (At) and tennessine (Ts) - that constitute Group 17 of the periodic table are called halogens.
● They were given the name halogen [hal- (“salt”) and -gen (“to produce”)] because they all produce sodium salts of similar properties, of which sodium chloride (table salt or halite) is best known.
Effect of Short-lived halogens on climate:
● Short-lived halogens, which have a lifetime of less than six months in the atmosphere, are naturally produced by the oceans. However, human activities have amplified their release into the atmosphere.
● However, their effect on methane is the opposite. Short-lived halogens increase methane’s lifetime in the atmosphere (causing a warming effect) by destroying hydroxyl radicals (OH). OH is a sink as it is known to break down methane. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Similarly, these halogens increase the levels of water vapour, a greenhouse gas, in the atmosphere, causing a warming effect. So, statement 2 is correct.
● These short-lived halogens reduce the formation of cooling aerosols, which are minute particles suspended in the atmosphere that reflect sunlight, causing a small warming effect. So, statement 3 is not correct.
● Short-lived halogens from oceans reduce warming by depleting ozone in the troposphere. Ozone is a greenhouse gas that traps outgoing radiation, leading to warming. So, statement 4 is not correct.
● Though these halogens drive an increase in warming by influencing methane, water vapour and aerosols, they compensate for this by destroying ozone, which exerts a cooling effect.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
The Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) is the maximum concentration of pesticide residue that is legally permissible in food and animal feeds as a result of pesticide use in accordance with Good Agricultural Practices
(GAP). In this context, consider the following statements:
1. The determination of MRLs on a global scale was instituted by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
2. In India, MRL are determined by the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI).
3. MRL cannot be prescribed for processed food commodities.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● The Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) is the maximum concentration of pesticide residue, given in mg/kg of the commodity, that is legally permissible in or on food and animal feeds as a result of pesticide use in accordance with Good Agricultural Practices (GAP).
● Under the joint FAO/WHO Food Standards Programme, the MRLs were established at the international level by the Codex Alimentarius Commission. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Furthermore, each country sets its own MRL values; for example, in India, MRLs are determined by the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, GOI, under the Prevention of Food Adulteration (PFA) Act 1954 (now Food Safety and Standards Authority, FSSA, 2006). So, statement 2 is correct.
● MRLs have been established for a variety of raw and processed food commodities. Foods can be of plant or animal origin, and they can be consumed by humans or animals. So, statement 3 is not correct. So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

q.t:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: Due to the existence of phytoplankton on the upper surface of the water the oceans appear green.
Statement-II: Chlorophyll absorbs the red and blue portions of the light spectrum and reflects green light.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

so.t:
● A recent study discovered that over half of the world's oceans are turning greener. While it is not certain about the exact cause of this change, it's evident that the oceans are shifting in colour and climate change is a significant factor responsible for this transformation.
● The oceans appear green, which happens due to the existence of phytoplankton on the upper surface of the water. So, statement 1 is correct.
○ Phytoplanktons are microscopic marine algae that contain the green-coloured pigment chlorophyll. ○ The pigment helps them absorb sunlight, which they use to capture carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and convert it into sugars. ○ Moreover, chlorophyll absorbs the red and blue portions of the light spectrum — or photosynthesis — and reflects green light. So, statement 2 is correct.
So, both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement I.

opt.t: they are the only insects that do not exhibit any kind of AMR
they carry genetic elements of key AMR drivers in their digestive tracts
they can directly consume antibiotics and become carriers of AMR genes
they can directly transmit AMR genes to humans

q.t: European Honeybees can act as an effective biomonitor to determine the spread of Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) in urban neighbourhoods because:

so.t:
● European honeybees can be an effective biomonitor for determining the spread of Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) in urban neighbourhoods.
● A recent paper has found bees can carry genetic elements of key AMR drivers in their digestive tracts.
○ AMR is the ability of bacteria and other microbes to resist the drugs used to inhibit or kill them.
● Bees interact with human environments, so they are a really good indicator of pollution that may present a risk of harm to humans.
● European honeybees, for example, can be a “crowdsourced” environmental proxy as they interact with contaminants in soil, dust, air, water and pollen while they forage. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to amphibians and reptiles:
1. Amphibians live in aquatic as well as terrestrial habitats while reptiles are mostly terrestrial.
2. Both undergo external fertilisation.
3. Both are viviparous and give birth to live offspring.
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● Amphibia: They can live in aquatic as well as terrestrial habitats. Most of them have two pairs of limbs. Body is divisible into the head and trunk.
● Reptilia: They are mostly terrestrial animals and their body is covered by dry and cornified skin, epidermal scales or scutes. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Amphibians have external fertilisation while reptiles have internal fertilisation. The process of internal fertilisation involves the sperm fertilising the egg inside the female. In an external fertilisation process, a male organism's sperm fertilises a female organism's egg outside of the female's body. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● Reptiles are oviparous and development is direct. While amphibians are also mostly oviparous and development is indirect. So, statement 3 is not correct.
○ Animals classified as oviparous lay eggs rather than directly giving birth to their progeny. Animals that are oviparous include amphibians, birds, and reptiles. Animals classified as viviparous give birth to live offspring rather than laying eggs. Animals such as humans, cats, dogs, etc. are viviparous. So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

opt.t: Flora that is characterised by enduring longevity and adaptability to diverse environmental conditions.
Plants that bloom only during specific seasons and have a short life cycle.
Flora that is resistant to pests and diseases, allowing them to thrive in harsh conditions.
Plants that require minimal water and can survive in arid environments for extended periods.

q.t: Which one of the following statements best describes the “ephemeral plants”?

so.t:
● Ephemeral species are plants and animals whose lifespan lasts only a few weeks or months.
● The most common types of ephemeral species are desert annuals, plants whose seeds remain dormant for months or years but which quickly germinate, grow, and flower when rain does fall.
● In such cases the amount and frequency of rainfall determine entirely how frequently ephemerals appear and how long they last.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

opt.t: distance between cyclonic origin and land surface
time taken between cyclonic origin and landfall

q.t: The classification of cyclonic disturbances over the North Indian Ocean by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) is based on:

so.t:
● A tropical cyclone (TC) is a rotational low-pressure system in tropics when the central pressure falls by 5 to 6 hPa from the surrounding and maximum sustained wind speed reaches 34 knots (about 62 kmph). It is a vast violent whirl of 150 to 800 km, spiraling around a centre and progressing along the surface of the sea at a rate of 300 to 500 km a day.
● The low-pressure systems over the Indian region are classified by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) based on the maximum sustained winds speed associated with the system and the pressure deficit/ number of closed isobars associated with the system.
● The pressure criteria are used when the system is over land and wind criteria is used, when the system is over the sea.
Criteria for classification of cyclonic disturbances over the North Indian Ocean

Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to Galaxies:
1. In Spiral galaxies, the youngest stars are formed in the spiral arms.
2. Elliptical galaxies usually contain less gas and dust than Spiral galaxies.
3. The Milky Way is a hyper active galaxy.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
● Galaxies can be characterised based on their shapes and physical features or by the activity in their central regions-powered by a supersized black hole or the angle at which we view them.
● Spiral Galaxies resemble giant rotating pinwheels with a pancake-like disk of stars and a central bulge or tight concentration of stars. The youngest stars form in gas-rich spiral arms, while older stars can be found throughout the disk and within the bulge and halo. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Elliptical galaxies are less common than spiral galaxies. They are characterized by their ellipsoidal shape and smooth, featureless image. They are made up of old red stars that have random orbits in multiple planes. The stars in these galaxies group into a ball, rather than as disc-like spiral galaxies. Unlike spirals, elliptical galaxies usually contain little gas and dust and show very little organisation or structure. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Irregular galaxies have unusual shapes, like toothpicks, rings, or little groupings of stars. Galaxies passing another with a stronger gravitational pull could lose some of its material, become distorted, and morph into a new shape.
● The Centers of Active galaxies appear more than 100 times brighter than the combined light of their stars. They can be spiral, elliptical, or irregular. The Milky Way is not currently an active galaxy. So, statement 3 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t: The Sutlej River flowed into the current channel of the Ghaggar-Hakra River until the 13th century after which it flowed into the Indus River due to plate tectonics. Which one of the following best describes the above geographical phenomenon?

so.t:
● River capture, also known as river piracy or stream piracy, is a geomorphological phenomenon that occurs when a river is diverted from its riverbed and starts flowing down the riverbed of another nearby river.
○ River flow directions and drainage systems are not necessarily permanent features of the landscape. Geological changes can cause a river to flow in a different direction and become a tributary of a different drainage system.
● The Sutlej River flowed into the current channel of the Ghaggar-Hakra River until the 13th century after which it flowed into the Indus River due to plate tectonics. River capture is a phenomenon in the Himalayan orogen, as reflected in numerous barbed drainages and asymmetric basins that extend across the orogen and into southern Tibet. Field evidence and a series of migrating knickpoints in the Zhada Basin of southern Tibet suggest late Pleistocene capture of the basin by the trans-Himalayan Sutlej River.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:

How many pairs given above are correctly matched?

so.t:
● The Kunchikal waterfall is India’s highest (455 metres) waterfall. It is located on the Shimoga-Udupi border in Karnataka. The waterfall is formed by the River Varahi. So, pair 1 is correctly matched.
● Barehipani waterfall is the highest waterfall in Odisha located within the Similipal Tiger Reserve. The local name is ‘Barehi’ which means Rope, which is how it looks. So, pair 2 is correctly matched.
● Nohkalikai is a rain fed waterfall and is located in Cherrapunji (Meghalaya). It is the tallest plunge waterfall in India. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
● Dudhsagar waterfall is perched in the high peaks of the Western Ghats in Goa. The waterfall appears like streams of milk rushing down the mountainside. So, pair 4 is not correctly matched. So, only three of the above pairs are correctly matched.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the South Atlantic Anomaly (SAA):
1. It is an area where the intensity of the Earth's magnetic field is very high.
2. It results from the flow of molten metals within the outer core of the Earth.
3. It impacts the satellites placed in the Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
How many of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
● South Atlantic Anomaly (SAA) is a region at the Earth’s surface where the intensity of the magnetic field is particularly low. It stretches out between South America and southwest Africa. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● SAA exists because the Earth's inner Van Allen radiation belt comes closest to the planet’s surface, causing an increased flux of energetic particles. This leads to the penetration of solar energetic particles deep into Earth’s atmosphere, posing severe problems for airplanes and ships' positioning systems as well as spacecraft electronic systems.
● The SAA arises from two features of Earth’s core: The tilt of its magnetic axis and the flow of molten metals within its outer core. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Currently, the SAA creates no visible impacts on daily life on the Earth's surface. Although the SAA arises from processes inside Earth, it has effects that reach far beyond Earth’s surface. The region can be hazardous for low-Earth orbit satellites that travel through it.
o If a satellite is hit by a high-energy proton, it can short-circuit and cause a Single Event Upset (SEU). This can cause the satellite’s function to glitch temporarily or can cause permanent damage if a key component is hit. So, statement 3 is correct. So, only two of the above statements are correct. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Which of the following components are included in the calculation of the Quota formula of the International Monetary Fund (IMF)?
1. Gross Domestic Product (GDP)
2. Openness to the global economy
3. International reserves
4. Economic equality
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
• The International Monetary Fund (IMF) is an international organisation that promotes global economic growth and financial stability, encourages international trade, and reduces poverty.
• Quota subscriptions are a central component of the IMF’s financial resources. Each IMF member country is assigned a quota, based broadly on its relative position in the world economy. A member country’s quota determines its maximum financial commitment to the IMF, voting power, and access to IMF financing.
o Votes comprise one vote per 100,000 Special Drawing Rights (SDR) of quota plus basic votes. SDRs are an international type of monetary reserve currency created by the IMF as a supplement to the existing money reserves of member countries.
• A member’s quota subscription determines the maximum amount of financial resources a member is obliged to provide to the IMF. A member must pay its subscription in full upon joining the Fund: up to 25 percent must be paid in SDRs or other members’ currencies specified by the IMF and the rest is paid in the member’s own currency.
• The current quota formula is a weighted average of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) (weight of 50 percent), openness to the global economy (30 percent), economic variability (15 percent) and international reserves (5 percent). So, points 1, 2 and 3 are correct.
o For this purpose, GDP is measured through a blend of GDP based on market exchange rates (weight of 60 percent) and on purchasing-power-parity exchange rates (40 percent).
o The formula also includes a “compression factor” that reduces the dispersion in calculated quota shares across members.
• Economic equality is not included in the formula. So, point 4 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding the SEHER program:
1. It is an initiative of NITI Aayog's Women Entrepreneurship Platform (WEP).
2. It is a comprehensive credit education program to enhance business skills among women entrepreneurs.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
● SEHER program: It was launched in 2024 and is an initiative of NITI Aayog's Women Entrepreneurship Platform (WEP) and TransUnion CIBIL. It is a comprehensive credit education program designed to enhance financial literacy and business skills among women entrepreneurs. This initiative is expected to enable women to access the financial tools necessary for business growth and job creation. So, statements 1 and 2 are correct.
● This initiative supports WEP’s goal of improving financing access for women in India’s 63 million MSMEs. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

q.t: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is planning to send its astronaut to the International Space Station (ISS) through which one of the following missions?

so.t:
● Axiom Mission 4 (or Ax-4) is a private spaceflight to the International Space Station. It is operated by Axiom Space and uses a SpaceX Crew Dragon spacecraft. The flight is in collaboration with NASA and will be the fourth flight of Axiom Space after Axiom Mission 1, Axiom Mission 2 and Axiom Mission 3. It is scheduled to launch in 2024.
● As a part of the Axiom-4 mission, the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has shortlisted two of its four trained Gaganyaan astronauts to travel to the International Space Station (ISS).
● It aims to train Indian astronauts in the operational aspects of the International Space Station (ISS). During their fourteen-day stay, the astronauts will gain hands-on experience with ISS modules and protocols. This mission is crucial for preparing Indian astronauts for future long-duration missions in space
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

q.t: The “Blue Line” is an internationally recognized border demarcation between:

so.t:
● The Blue Line is an internationally recognized border demarcation established in 2000 by the United Nations to confirm the withdrawal of Israeli forces from Lebanon.
● It serves as a boundary between Israel and Lebanon and has been a point of tension and conflict, especially involving the presence of UN peacekeepers and the Hezbollah militia.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

ENGLISH.desc:
Please read the following instructions very carefully:

1. You have 120 minutes to complete the test.
2. The test contains a total of 100 questions And 200 Marks.
3. There is only one correct answer to each question. Click on the most appropriate option to mark it as your answer.
4. You will be awarded 2 for each correct answer.
5. There is no penalty for wrong answer.
6. You can change your answer by clicking on some other option.
7. You can unmark your answer by clicking on the “Clear Response” button.
8. A Number list of all questions appears at the right hand side of the screen. You can access the questions in any order within a section or across sections by clicking on the question number given on the number list.
9. You can use rough sheets while taking the test. Do not use calculators, log tables, dictionaries, or any other printed/online reference material during the test.
10. Do not click the button “Submit test” before completing the test. A test once submitted cannot be resumed.
